children.text: to establish a grant program to support schools that use Native American and Alaska Native languages as the primary language of instruction;

to maintain, protect, and promote the rights and freedom of Native Americans and Alaska Natives to use, practice, maintain, and revitalize their languages, as envisioned in the Native American Languages Act ( et seq.); and25 U.S.C. 2901

to support the Nation’s First Peoples’ efforts to maintain and revitalize their languages and cultures, and to improve educational opportunities and student outcomes within Native American and Alaska Native communities.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: From funds reserved under , the Secretary shall reserve 20 percent to make grants to eligible entities to develop and maintain, or to improve and expand, programs that support schools, including elementary school and secondary school education sites and streams, using Native American and Alaska Native languages as the primary languages of instruction.section 7492(c) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: An Indian tribe.

children.label: B
children.text: A Tribal College or University (as defined in ).section 1059c of this title

children.label: C
children.text: A tribal education agency.

children.label: D
children.text: A local educational agency, including a public charter school that is a local educational agency under State law.

children.label: E
children.text: A school operated by the Bureau of Indian Education.

children.label: F
children.text: An Alaska Native Regional Corporation (as described in ).section 1602(g) of title 43

children.label: G
children.text: A private, tribal, or Alaska Native nonprofit organization.

children.label: H
children.text: A nontribal for-profit organization.

children.label: A
children.text: The name of the Native American or Alaska Native language to be used for instruction at the school supported by the eligible entity.

children.label: B
children.text: The number of students attending such school.

children.label: C
children.text: The number of hours of instruction in or through 1 or more Native American or Alaska Native languages being provided to targeted students at such school, if any.

children.label: i
children.text: use the funds provided to meet the purposes of this section;

children.label: ii
children.text: implement the activities described in subsection (e);

children.label: iii
children.text: ensure the implementation of rigorous academic content; and

children.label: iv
children.text: ensure that students progress toward high-level fluency goals.

children.label: i
children.text: the school governing entity (such as a local educational agency, tribal education agency or department, charter organization, private organization, or other governing entity);

children.label: ii
children.text: the school’s accreditation status;

children.label: iii
children.text: any partnerships with institutions of higher education; and

children.label: iv
children.text: any indigenous language schooling and research cooperatives.

children.label: i
children.text: the school is engaged in meeting State or tribally designated long-term goals for students, as may be required by applicable Federal, State, or tribal law;

children.label: ii
children.text: the school provides assessments of students using the Native American or Alaska Native language of instruction, where possible;

children.label: iii
children.text: the qualifications of all instructional and leadership personnel at such school is sufficient to deliver high-quality education through the Native American or Alaska Native language used in the school; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the school will collect and report to the public data relative to student achievement and, if appropriate, rates of high school graduation, career readiness, and enrollment in postsecondary education or workforce development programs, of students who are enrolled in the school’s programs.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: The Secretary shall not give a priority in awarding grants under this section based on the information described in paragraph (1)(E).

children.label: i
children.text: the school or organization has the capacity to provide education primarily through a Native American or an Alaska Native language; and

children.label: ii
children.text: there are sufficient speakers of the target language at the school or available to be hired by the school or organization.

children.label: i
children.text: A Tribal College or University (as defined in ).section 1059c of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: A Federally recognized Indian tribe or tribal organization.

children.label: iii
children.text: An Alaska Native Regional Corporation or an Alaska Native nonprofit organization.

children.label: iv
children.text: A Native Hawaiian organization.
determine the amount of each grant and the duration of each grant, which shall not exceed 3 years; and
ensure, to the maximum extent feasible, that diversity in languages is represented.

children.label: A
children.text: Supporting Native American or Alaska Native language education and development.

children.label: B
children.text: Providing professional development for teachers and, as appropriate, staff and administrators to strengthen the overall language and academic goals of the school that will be served by the grant program.

children.label: A
children.text: Developing or refining curriculum, including teaching materials and activities, as appropriate.

children.label: B
children.text: Creating or refining assessments written in the Native American or Alaska Native language of instruction that measure student proficiency and that are aligned with State or tribal academic standards.

children.label: C
children.text: Carrying out other activities that promote the maintenance and revitalization of the Native American or Alaska Native language relevant to the grant program.
the activities the entity carried out to meet the purposes of this section; and
the number of children served by the program and the number of instructional hours in the Native American or Alaska Native language.

children.heading: Administrative costs
children.label: g Administrative costs
children.text: Not more than 5 percent of the funds provided to a grantee under this section for any fiscal year may be used for administrative purposes.
